title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the failure-atomic resource constructor to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of failure-atomic resource constructor with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Partial Initialization State
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 7927. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.

What must be established before implementation?

stimulus.title: Error Cause Preservation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Error Cause Preservation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Error Cause Preservation case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Error Cause Preservation
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Error Cause Preservation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 1260. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Error Cause Preservation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Error Cause Preservation, keep Error Cause Preservation connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.
